source_paper_title: An Stochastic Differential Equation Perspective on Stochastic Convex Optimization
source_paper_bibtex: @article{Rodrigo_2025, title={An Stochastic Differential Equation Perspective on Stochastic Convex Optimization}, volume={50}, ISSN={1526-5471}, url={http://dx.doi.org/10.1287/moor.2022.0162}, DOI={10.1287/moor.2022.0162}, number={4}, journal={Mathematics of Operations Research}, publisher={Institute for Operations Research and the Management Sciences (INFORMS)}, author={Rodrigo, Maulén S. and Fadili, Jalal and Attouch, Hedy}, year={2025}, month=nov, pages={3190–3221} }
context:
Page 23, Section 4.2 (Remark 4.10 and Conjecture 4.11).

To the best of our knowledge, there is no way to arithmetically solve this non linear ODE, not even a sharp bound of the solution. Nevertheless, if \(y(t)=O\big((t+1)^{-\frac{1}{b-1}}\big)\), then \(p_\delta(t)=O\big((t+1)^{-\frac{b}{b-1}}\big)\). Which leads us to make the following conjecture: Conjecture 4.11. If \(p_\delta=O(\sigma_\infty^2)\) and \(\sigma_\infty^2(t)=O\big((t+1)^{-\frac{b}{b-1}}\big)\) (for constants independent of \(\delta\) and \(\hat t_\delta\)), then \(y(t)=O\big((t+1)^{-\frac{1}{b-1}}\big)\).
title: Prove sharp decay rates for nonlinear comparison ODE driven by SDE noise
background_latex:
Let \(f:\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}\) be convex and continuously differentiable with \(L\)-Lipschitz gradient, and assume the minimizer set \(S=\operatorname{argmin} f\neq\emptyset\). Consider the stochastic differential equation (SDE)
\[
 dX(t)=-\nabla f(X(t))\,dt+\sigma(t,X(t))\,dW(t),\qquad t\ge 0,
\]
where \(W\) is an \(m\)-dimensional Brownian motion and \(\sigma:\mathbb{R}_+\times\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}^{d\times m}\) is measurable, globally bounded in Frobenius norm, and Lipschitz in \(x\) uniformly in \(t\). Define the worst-case diffusion magnitude
\[
\sigma_\infty(t):=\sup_{x\in\mathbb{R}^d}\|\sigma(t,x)\|_F.
\]
Assume \(\sigma_\infty\in L^2(\mathbb{R}_+)\), so the noise vanishes in a square-integrable way.

Assume further that \(f\) satisfies a local error-bound / \L ojasiewicz-type condition near \(S\), leading (after localization arguments) to differential inequalities for certain nonnegative quantities \(y(t)\) (e.g., \(y(t)=\mathbb{E}[f(X(t)) - \min f]\) restricted to high-probability events, or \(y(t)=\mathbb{E}[\mathrm{dist}(X(t),S)^2/2]\) similarly restricted).

In the analysis, one is led to study a nonlinear scalar ODE (comparison equation)
\[
 y'(t)=-a\,y(t)^b+p_\delta(t),\qquad t>\hat t_\delta,
\]
with parameters \(a>0\), \(b>1\), an initial condition \(y(\hat t_\delta)=y_0(\hat t_\delta,\delta)>0\), and a nonnegative forcing term \(p_\delta(t)\) depending on the noise profile. The forcing term is of the same order as \(\sigma_\infty(t)^2\) (more precisely, \(p_\delta(t)=O(\sigma_\infty(t)^2)\) in the regime of interest), but it may also depend on \(\delta\) and \(\hat t_\delta\) through the localization step (Egorov-type arguments).
problem_definition_latex:
Assume \(a>0\), \(b>1\), and let \(p_\delta:[\hat t_\delta,\infty)\to\mathbb{R}_+\) be a measurable function satisfying \(p_\delta(t)=O(\sigma_\infty(t)^2)\). Assume also that
\[
\sigma_\infty(t)^2 = O\big((t+1)^{-\frac{b}{b-1}}\big)\qquad (t\to\infty),
\]
with constants independent of \(\delta\) and \(\hat t_\delta\). Consider the ODE
\[
 y'(t)=-a\,y(t)^b+p_\delta(t),\qquad t>\hat t_\delta,\qquad y(\hat t_\delta)=y_0(\hat t_\delta,\delta)>0.
\]
Establish that the corresponding solution satisfies the decay rate
\[
 y(t)=O\big((t+1)^{-\frac{1}{b-1}}\big)\qquad (t\to\infty).
\]
(Equivalently: prove the stated implication between the forcing decay \(p_\delta(t)\) at order \((t+1)^{-b/(b-1)}\) and the solution decay \(y(t)\) at order \((t+1)^{-1/(b-1)}\), uniformly with respect to the localization parameters \(\delta\) and \(\hat t_\delta\) as above.)
latex:

where \(W\) is an \(m\)-dimensional Brownian motion and \(\sigma:\mathbb{R}_+\times\mathbb{R}^d\to\mathbb{R}^{d\times m}\) is measurable, globally bounded in Frobenius norm, and Lipschitz in \(x\) uniformly in \(t\). Define the worst-case diffusion magnitude
\[
\sigma_\infty(t):=\sup_{x\in\mathbb{R}^d}\|\sigma(t,x)\|_F.
\]

Assume further that \(f\) satisfies a local error-bound / \L ojasiewicz-type condition near \(S\), leading (after localization arguments) to differential inequalities for certain nonnegative quantities \(y(t)\) (e.g., \(y(t)=\mathbb{E}[f(X(t)) - \min f]\) restricted to high-probability events, or \(y(t)=\mathbb{E}[\mathrm{dist}(X(t),S)^2/2]\) similarly restricted).

\[

with parameters \(a>0\), \(b>1\), an initial condition \(y(\hat t_\delta)=y_0(\hat t_\delta,\delta)>0\), and a nonnegative forcing term \(p_\delta(t)\) depending on the noise profile. The forcing term is of the same order as \(\sigma_\infty(t)^2\) (more precisely, \(p_\delta(t)=O(\sigma_\infty(t)^2)\) in the regime of interest), but it may also depend on \(\delta\) and \(\hat t_\delta\) through the localization step (Egorov-type arguments).

Assume \(a>0\), \(b>1\), and let \(p_\delta:[\hat t_\delta,\infty)\to\mathbb{R}_+\) be a measurable function satisfying \(p_\delta(t)=O(\sigma_\infty(t)^2)\). Assume also that
\[
\sigma_\infty(t)^2 = O\big((t+1)^{-\frac{b}{b-1}}\big)\qquad (t\to\infty),

with constants independent of \(\delta\) and \(\hat t_\delta\). Consider the ODE
\[

\]

\[

\]
(Equivalently: prove the stated implication between the forcing decay \(p_\delta(t)\) at order \((t+1)^{-b/(b-1)}\) and the solution decay \(y(t)\) at order \((t+1)^{-1/(b-1)}\), uniformly with respect to the localization parameters \(\delta\) and \(\hat t_\delta\) as above.)
open_problem_quote: To the best of our knowledge, there is no way to arithmetically solve this non linear ODE, not even a sharp bound of the solution. Nevertheless, if \(y(t)=O\big((t+1)^{-\frac{1}{b-1}}\big)\), then \(p_\delta(t)=O\big((t+1)^{-\frac{b}{b-1}}\big)\). Which leads us to make the following conjecture: Conjecture 4.11. If \(p_\delta=O(\sigma_\infty^2)\) and \(\sigma_\infty^2(t)=O\big((t+1)^{-\frac{b}{b-1}}\big)\) (for constants independent of \(\delta\) and \(\hat t_\delta\)), then \(y(t)=O\big((t+1)^{-\frac{1}{b-1}}\big)\).
counterexample_suitability_rationale: The statement is an asymptotic implication for all forcing terms within a big-O class, so finite-dimensional counterexample search is not directly applicable without discretizing an infinite function space and specifying uniformity precisely.
alphaevolve_suitability_rationale: One can numerically explore families of forcings \(p_\delta\) and simulate the ODE to test candidate rate bounds and identify worst-case behaviors, but translating numerical evidence into a rigorous uniform bound (especially with dependence on \(\delta,\hat t_\delta\)) remains nontrivial.

verification.notice: Generated from the paper text only and not independently verified as still open.
